summary: This source outlines the connection between early-life psychopathology and accelerated biological aging. Using data from the Dunedin Longitudinal Study, Moffitt and Caspi demonstrate that a higher general liability to mental disorders (the p factor) correlates with a faster pace of biological aging across multiple bodily systems, suggesting psychiatry has a crucial role in preventing age-related diseases.

principles.statement: Early-life mental disorders are a precursor to later-life physical and neurodegenerative diseases.
principles.rationale: Epidemiological and longitudinal data show an overlap where early-onset psychiatric conditions predict the development of non-infectious physical diseases later in life.
principles.source_quote: Young people who experience mental disorder tend to develop non-infectious and neurodegenerative diseases as they age.

principles.statement: Experiencing a higher burden of psychopathology accelerates the biological aging process.
principles.rationale: Longitudinal tracking of biomarkers across multiple bodily systems reveals that individuals with higher general psychopathology scores experience a faster rate of physiological decline per chronological year.
principles.source_quote: individuals in the Dunedin Longitudinal Study who experience more psychopathology tend to age faster.

mental_models.name: The p factor
mental_models.description: A latent variable that operationalizes an individual's general liability to experience any and all mental disorders over their life-course. It summarizes the common variance among multiple psychiatric symptoms.
mental_models.example: Measuring the p factor by summarizing all psychiatric symptoms ever experienced by Dunedin cohort members between ages 18 and 38 to predict their later pace of aging.

mental_models.name: Pace of Aging
mental_models.description: A geroscience concept that operationalizes aging not just as chronological time, but as the coordinated, progressive loss of integrity across multiple bodily systems (e.g., cardiovascular, metabolic, immune).
mental_models.example: Quantifying the rate of biological aging in year-units of physiological decline per chronological year using 18 biomarkers measured at ages 26, 32, and 38.

signature_quotes.text: Young people who experience mental disorder tend to develop non-infectious and neurodegenerative diseases as they age.
signature_quotes.context: Explaining the trajectory from early psychiatric vulnerability to later-life physical disease.

signature_quotes.text: The p factor operationalizes the theory of liability to experience any and all mental disorders over the life-course.
signature_quotes.context: Defining the p factor in the context of the Dunedin Longitudinal Study.

signature_quotes.text: The Pace of Aging operationalizes the coordinated progressive loss of integrity across bodily systems that is the aging process according to geroscience theory.
signature_quotes.context: Defining how biological aging is measured across multiple physiological systems.
